session.user:
packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/plan_header.py
this looks like it trades in raw things which are parsed and produced repeatedly. is it time to introduce an intermediate data structure?
session.tool_result: File content (14705 tokens) exceeds maximum allowed tokens (10000). Use offset and limit parameters to read specific portions of the file, or search for specific content instead of reading the whole file.

1→"""Schema v2 plan header operations for erk-plan issues. 2→ 3→These support the new plan issue structure where: 4→- Issue body contains only compact metadata (for fast querying) 5→- First comment contains the plan content 6→- last_dispatched_run_id is stored in issue body 7→ 8→Optional fields (added over time, backward compatible): 9→- source_repo: For cross-repo plans, the repo where this plan will be 10→ implemented (e.g., "owner/impl-repo"). When set, the plan issue lives in 11→ a different repo (the plans repo) than where code changes will be made. 12→""" 13→ 14→import re 15→from typing import Any 16→ 17→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.core import ( 18→ create_metadata_block, 19→ create_plan_body_block, 20→ find_metadata_block, 21→ render_metadata_block, 22→ render_plan_body_block, 23→ replace_metadata_block_in_body, 24→) 25→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.schemas import ( 26→ BRANCH_NAME, 27→ CI_SUMMARY_COMMENT_ID, 28→ CREATED_AT, 29→ CREATED_BY, 30→ CREATED_FROM_SESSION,
... [70 lines omitted] ...

100→ Args: 101→ created_at: ISO 8601 timestamp of plan creation 102→ created_by: GitHub username of plan creator 103→ worktree_name: Optional worktree name (set when worktree is created) 104→ branch_name: Optional git branch name for this plan 105→ plan_comment_id: Optional GitHub comment ID containing plan content 106→ last_dispatched_run_id: Optional workflow run ID (set by workflow) 107→ last_dispatched_node_id: Optional GraphQL node ID (set by workflow, for batch queries) 108→ last_dispatched_at: Optional dispatch timestamp (set by workflow) 109→ last_local_impl_at: Optional local implementation timestamp (set by plan-implement) 110→ last_local_impl_event: Optional event type ("started" or "ended") 111→ last_local_impl_session: Optional Claude Code session ID 112→ last_local_impl_user: Optional user who ran implementation 113→ last_remote_impl_at: Optional remote implementation timestamp (set by GitHub Actions) 114→ last_remote_impl_run_id: Optional GitHub Actions run ID for remote implementation 115→ last_remote_impl_session_id: Optional Claude Code session ID for remote implementation 116→ source_repo: For cross-repo plans, the repo where implementation happens 117→ objective_issue: Optional parent objective issue number 118→ node_ids: Optional list of objective roadmap node IDs covered by this plan 119→ created_from_session: Optional session ID that created this plan 120→ created_from_workflow_run_url: Optional workflow run URL that created this plan 121→ last_learn_session: Optional session ID that last invoked learn 122→ last_learn_at: Optional ISO 8601 timestamp of last learn invocation 123→ learn_status: Optional learning workflow status 124→ learn_plan_issue: Optional issue number of generated learn plan 125→ learn_plan_pr: Optional PR number that implemented the learn plan 126→ learned_from_issue: Optional parent plan issue number (for learn plans) 127→ lifecycle_stage: Optional lifecycle stage for the plan 128→ 129→ Returns:
... [70 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Core data structures and constants for GitHub metadata blocks.""" 2→ 3→from abc import ABC, abstractmethod 4→from dataclasses import dataclass, field 5→from typing import Any 6→ 7→ 8→class BlockKeys: 9→ """Canonical metadata block key constants. 10→ 11→ Import this class instead of hardcoding string literals:: 12→ 13→ from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.types import BlockKeys 14→ 15→ find_metadata_block(body, BlockKeys.PLAN_HEADER) 16→ """ 17→ 18→ PLAN_HEADER = "plan-header" 19→ PLAN_BODY = "plan-body" 20→ OBJECTIVE_HEADER = "objective-header" 21→ OBJECTIVE_BODY = "objective-body" 22→ OBJECTIVE_ROADMAP = "objective-roadmap" 23→ ERK_PLAN = "erk-plan" 24→ ERK_WORKTREE_CREATION = "erk-worktree-creation" 25→ ERK_IMPLEMENTATION_STATUS = "erk-implementation-status" 26→ WORKFLOW_STARTED = "workflow-started" 27→ SUBMISSION_QUEUED = "submission-queued" 28→ PLAN_RETRY = "plan-retry" 29→ IMPL_STARTED = "impl-started" 30→ IMPL_ENDED = "impl-ended"
... [55 lines omitted] ...
53→class MetadataBlockError: 54→ """A metadata block that failed to parse.""" 62→ """Result of parsing metadata blocks, with explicit error reporting.""" 65→ errors: tuple[MetadataBlockError, ...] 69→ def has_errors(self) -> bool: 70→ return bool(self.errors) 78→ """Validate data against schema. Raises ValueError if invalid."""

1→"""Schema implementations for metadata blocks.""" 2→ 3→import warnings 4→from dataclasses import dataclass 5→from typing import Any, Literal 6→ 7→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.types import BlockKeys, MetadataBlockSchema 8→ 9→ 10→def _migrate_to_pr_number(data: dict[str, Any]) -> None: 11→ """Migrate legacy issue_number or plan_number to pr_number in-place. 12→ 13→ Several metadata schemas originally used ``issue_number`` or ``plan_number``. 14→ This helper centralises the backward-compatibility migration so each schema's 15→ ``validate`` method can call it instead of duplicating the logic. 16→ """ 17→ if "issue_number" in data and "pr_number" not in data: 18→ data["pr_number"] = data.pop("issue_number") 19→ if "plan_number" in data and "pr_number" not in data: 20→ data["pr_number"] = data.pop("plan_number") 21→ 22→ 23→@dataclass(frozen=True) 24→class ImplementationStatusSchema(MetadataBlockSchema): 25→ """Schema for erk-implementation-status blocks (completion status).""" 26→ 27→ def validate(self, data: dict[str, Any]) -> None: 28→ """Validate erk-implementation-status data structure.""" 29→ required_fields = { 30→ "status",
... [70 lines omitted] ...

1→"""GitHub draft PR implementation of managed PR backend. 2→ 3→Uses GitHub draft pull requests as the backing store for managed PRs. 4→PR body contains the plan-header metadata block followed by plan content. 5→The "[erk-pr]" title prefix identifies managed PRs vs regular draft PRs. 6→""" 7→ 8→import logging 9→from collections.abc import Mapping 10→from datetime import UTC 11→from pathlib import Path 12→ 13→from erk_shared.gateway.github.abc import LocalGitHub 14→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.abc import GitHubIssues 15→from erk_shared.gateway.github.label_ops import add_labels_resilient 16→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.core import ( 17→ add_metadata_block, 18→ find_metadata_block, 19→ has_metadata_block, 20→ render_metadata_block, 21→ replace_metadata_block_in_body, 22→) 23→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.plan_header import ( 24→ format_plan_header_body, 25→) 26→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.schemas import ( 27→ CREATED_FROM_SESSION, 28→ CREATED_FROM_WORKFLOW_RUN_URL, 29→ LEARNED_FROM_ISSUE, 30→ NODE_IDS,

session.assistant:
Yes, this file is screaming for a `PlanHeaderData` intermediate. Here's what I see:
**The problems:**
1. **Repeated parsing**: Every `extract_plan_header_*` function re-parses `find_metadata_block(issue_body, BlockKeys.PLAN_HEADER)`. If a caller needs 3 fields, it parses YAML 3 times.
2. **30-arg passthrough**: `create_plan_header_block` takes 30 kwargs, and `format_plan_header_body` takes the identical 30 kwargs just to forward them. Callers like `planned_pr.py:333` and `planned_pr.py:658` pass 30 args with most set to `None`.
3. **Untyped dict access**: All field reads go through `block.data.get(STRING_KEY)` returning `Any`. No type safety, no autocomplete.
4. **Duplicated update boilerplate**: Every `update_plan_header_*` function repeats the same 7-line pattern: find block, copy dict, set field(s), validate, create block, render, replace.
**The fix — a frozen dataclass:**
```python @dataclass(frozen=True) class PlanHeaderData: created_at: str created_by: str worktree_name: str | None branch_name: str | None plan_comment_id: int | None # ... all ~30 fields with explicit types ...
@classmethod def from_issue_body(cls, issue_body: str) -> PlanHeaderData | None: """Parse once, access many.""" block = find_metadata_block(issue_body, BlockKeys.PLAN_HEADER) if block is None: return None return cls( created_at=block.data.get(CREATED_AT, ""), created_by=block.data.get(CREATED_BY, ""), # ... )
def to_metadata_block(self) -> MetadataBlock: """Render back to MetadataBlock.""" # ...
def render_body(self) -> str: """Render to issue body string.""" # ... ```
**What this buys you:**
- **Parse once**: `header = PlanHeaderData.from_issue_body(body)` then `header.worktree_name`, `header.branch_name` — no re-parsing - **Type-safe field access**: `header.objective_issue` is `int | None`, not `Any` - **Updates via `dataclasses.replace`**: `updated = dataclasses.replace(header, worktree_name="new")` — replaces all the `update_plan_header_*` functions with a one-liner + render - **Callers pass one object**: Instead of 30 kwargs, callers construct a `PlanHeaderData` and pass it around - **The 20+ extract functions and 10+ update functions mostly collapse into the dataclass** itself
The existing `extract_*` and `update_*` functions could stay as thin wrappers during migration (taking `issue_body: str` for backwards compat), but the core logic moves to the dataclass. Eventually callers migrate to holding `PlanHeaderData` directly.
Want me to plan or implement this?
I'll explore the codebase to understand the full scope of this refactoring. Let me launch parallel explorations.

session.tool_result:
Excellent! Now I have enough information. Let me compile a comprehensive report. Based on my analysis, here's what I found:
## Comprehensive Caller Analysis for plan_header.py
I've identified all **20 files** that import from `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/plan_header.py`. Here's the complete breakdown:
### PRODUCTION CODE (8 files)
#### 1. **packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_store/planned_pr.py** - **Imports:** `format_plan_header_body` - **Usage:** Called **twice** with the full 23-argument signature (all optional fields passed as keyword args) - Line ~150: Creating metadata body for new PR creation workflow - Line ~175: Creating metadata body for cross-repo plan conversion - **Pattern:** Constructs `format_plan_header_body()` inline with explicit arguments; passes all fields
#### 2. **src/erk/cli/commands/consolidate_learn_plans_dispatch.py** - **Imports:** `format_plan_header_body` - **Usage:** Single call with full 23-argument signature - **Pattern:** One-shot dispatch creating plan PR; constructs inline
#### 3. **src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot_remote_dispatch.py** - **Imports:** `format_plan_header_body` - **Usage:** Single call with full 23-argument signature - **Pattern:** One-shot dispatch for generic prompts; constructs inline
#### 4. **src/erk/cli/commands/pr/dispatch_cmd.py** - **Imports:** `extract_plan_header_branch_name` - **Usage:** Single extraction of one field from issue_body - **Pattern:** Retrieves branch name to verify dispatch state
... [192 lines omitted] ...

Perfect! Now I have enough information to provide a comprehensive report. Let me compile the findings:
## Test Coverage Summary for `plan_header.py`
Based on my analysis of the codebase, here's the comprehensive test coverage:
### Test Files (7 files, 149 test functions total)
#### 1. **tests/shared/github/test_plan_header_extraction.py** (93 tests) - **Location**: `/Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-14/tests/shared/github/test_plan_header_extraction.py` - **Scope**: Pure unit tests for plan header functions with zero dependencies - **Coverage**: - Schema validation (accepts/rejects various field combinations) - Block creation (`create_plan_header_block`, `format_plan_header_body`) - Extraction functions (`extract_plan_header_*` for all fields) - Update functions (`update_plan_header_*` for all fields) - Round-trip rendering and parsing - **Key Test Patterns**: - Schema acceptance tests (minimal data, optional fields, cross-repo plans) - Schema rejection tests (missing required fields, invalid node_ids) - Block creation with optional/required fields - Field extraction with fallback to None - Learn workflow fields (last_learn_session, last_learn_at, learn_status, learn_plan_issue, learn_plan_pr, learned_from_issue) - Remote implementation fields (last_remote_impl_run_id, last_remote_impl_session_id) - CI summary comment ID tracking
#### 2. **tests/shared/github/test_metadata_extraction.py** (26 tests) - **Location**: `/Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-14/tests/shared/github/test_metadata_extraction.py` - **Scope**: Tests for metadata block extraction and update functions - **Coverage**:
... [139 lines omitted] ...
- Error handling (missing plan-header block) - Error handling (missing plan-header block) - Error cases (plan not found, no plan-header block, no fields provided) **Pattern 5: Error Testing** - Tests expect `ValueError` with specific messages - Patterns: Updates field, raises ValueError if block missing, preserves other fields

Perfect! Now I have comprehensive information. Let me compile the findings:
## Summary Report: Frozen Dataclass Patterns and PlanHeaderSchema Analysis
### 1. Existing Frozen Dataclass Patterns in `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/`
**Pattern: Simple Immutable Data Containers** Frozen dataclasses are used throughout the codebase for immutable intermediate data structures:
- **examples/naming.py**: `ValidPrTitle`, `InvalidPrTitle`, `ValidWorktreeName`, `InvalidWorktreeName`, `ValidObjectiveSlug`, `InvalidObjectiveSlug` — discriminated unions for validation results (frozen fields, no factory methods) - **plan_store/types.py**: `Plan`, `PlanState`, `PlanQuery`, `CreatePlanResult`, `PrNotFound` — provider-agnostic plan representations with field defaults where needed (e.g., `header_fields: dict = field(default_factory=dict)`) - **context/types.py**: `RepoContext`, `NoRepoSentinel`, `InteractiveAgentConfig` — configuration and context objects with static factory methods (`default()`, `with_overrides()`)
**Key Pattern Characteristics:** - Fields use plain names (no underscores) - Optional fields use `| None` with no defaults (or `field(default_factory=...)` for collections) - Static factory methods like `default()` or `@staticmethod` methods are used instead of `@classmethod from_*` patterns - `__post_init__` can be used for frozen dataclass initialization logic (see `RepoContext` using `object.__setattr__` to modify frozen fields during init) - No underscore-prefixed internal fields (those are reserved for slots-based ABC implementations per conventions.md)
### 2. PlanHeaderSchema Fields (Complete List with Types)
**Location**: `/Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-14/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/schemas.py`
**Required Fields (3):** - `schema_version: str` — literal "2" - `created_at: str` — ISO 8601 timestamp - `created_by: str` — GitHub username
**Optional Fields (31) - All support `None`:**
... [113 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant: Good exploration results. Let me read a few critical files to verify key details before designing.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: Code Conventions 3→read_when: 4→ - "naming functions or variables" 5→ - "creating CLI commands" 6→ - "naming Claude artifacts" 7→ - "moving code between packages" 8→ - "creating imports" 9→ - "creating immutable classes or frozen dataclasses" 10→ - "implementing an ABC with abstract properties" 11→tripwires: 12→ - action: "writing `__all__` to a Python file" 13→ warning: "Re-export modules are forbidden. Import directly from where code is defined." 14→ - action: "adding --force flag to a CLI command" 15→ warning: 'Always include -f as the short form. Pattern: @click.option("-f", "--force", ...)' 16→ - action: "adding a function with 5+ parameters" 17→ warning: "Load `dignified-python` skill first. Use keyword-only arguments (add `*` after first param). Exception: ABC/Protocol method signatures and Click command callbacks." 18→ - action: "editing source files directly on master branch" 19→ warning: "Never edit source files on master. Even for one-line fixes, use plan-first workflow. Bypasses review, CI gates, and worktree isolation." 20→last_audited: "2026-02-16 14:20 PT" 21→audit_result: clean 22→--- 23→ 24→# Code Conventions 25→ 26→This document defines conventions that supplement AGENTS.md. For basic naming (snake_case, PascalCase, kebab-case), Claude artifacts naming, brand names, worktree terminology, and CLI command organization, see AGENTS.md directly. 27→ 28→## Variable Naming by Type 29→ 30→| Type | Convention | Example |

1→"""Core types for provider-agnostic plan storage.""" 2→ 3→from dataclasses import dataclass, field 4→from datetime import datetime 5→from enum import Enum 6→ 7→ 8→class PlanState(Enum): 9→ """State of a plan.""" 10→ 11→ OPEN = "OPEN" 12→ CLOSED = "CLOSED" 13→ 14→ 15→@dataclass(frozen=True) 16→class Plan: 17→ """Provider-agnostic representation of a plan. 18→ 19→ Fields: 20→ pr_identifier: Provider-specific ID as string 21→ (GitHub: "42", Jira: "PROJ-123", Linear: UUID) 22→ title: Plan title 23→ body: Plan body/description 24→ state: Plan state (OPEN or CLOSED) 25→ url: Web URL to view the plan 26→ labels: List of label names 27→ assignees: List of assignee usernames 28→ created_at: Creation timestamp 29→ updated_at: Last update timestamp 30→ metadata: Provider-specific fields (e.g., {"number": 42} for GitHub)
... [59 lines omitted] ...
80→class PlanHeaderNotFoundError(RuntimeError):

1→"""Helpers for creating PR backends with Plan objects in tests. 2→ 3→This module provides utilities for tests that need to set up plan state. 4→It converts Plan objects to ManagedGitHubPrBackend backed by FakeLocalGitHub. 5→""" 6→ 7→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.types import IssueInfo 8→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.plan_header import format_plan_header_body 9→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import PRDetails, PullRequestInfo 10→from erk_shared.plan_store.planned_pr import ManagedGitHubPrBackend 11→from erk_shared.plan_store.planned_pr_lifecycle import ( 12→ DETAILS_CLOSE, 13→ DETAILS_OPEN, 14→) 15→from erk_shared.plan_store.types import Plan, PlanState 16→from tests.fakes.gateway.github import FakeLocalGitHub 17→from tests.fakes.gateway.github_issues import FakeGitHubIssues 18→from tests.fakes.gateway.time import FakeTime 19→ 20→_PLAN_HEADER_END_MARKER = "<!-- /erk:metadata-block:plan-header -->" 21→ 22→ 23→def _plan_to_pr_details(plan: Plan) -> PRDetails: 24→ """Convert a Plan to PRDetails for FakeLocalGitHub. 25→ 26→ Handles two body formats: 27→ 1. Body with plan-header metadata block: reformats into PR body format 28→ (metadata block + separator + plan content) 29→ 2. Plain body without metadata: uses body directly as PR body 30→

session.assistant: Interesting — `Plan` already has `header_fields: dict[str, object]` as a pre-parsed cache. Let me check the conversion code.
session.tool_result:
1→"""Shared conversion helpers for Plan construction from provider-specific types. 2→ 3→Provides single-parse conversions that populate header_fields on Plan, 4→eliminating repeated YAML parsing of the plan-header metadata block. 5→Also provides typed accessor helpers for reading fields from header_fields. 6→""" 7→ 8→from datetime import datetime 9→ 10→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.types import IssueInfo 11→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.core import find_metadata_block 12→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.schemas import NODE_IDS, OBJECTIVE_ISSUE 13→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.types import BlockKeys 14→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import PRDetails 15→from erk_shared.plan_store.types import Plan, PlanState 16→ 17→ 18→def _parse_node_ids(value: object) -> tuple[str, ...] | None: 19→ """Parse node_ids from header_fields value. 20→ 21→ Args: 22→ value: Raw value from header_fields (list of strings, or None) 23→ 24→ Returns: 25→ Tuple of node ID strings, or None if not present 26→ """ 27→ if value is None: 28→ return None 29→ if isinstance(value, list) and all(isinstance(item, str) for item in value): 30→ return tuple(str(item) for item in value)
... [172 lines omitted] ...

100→ """Schema for erk-plan blocks.""" 101→ 102→ def validate(self, data: dict[str, Any]) -> None: 103→ """Validate erk-plan data structure.""" 104→ _migrate_to_pr_number(data) 105→ 106→ required_fields = {"pr_number", "worktree_name", "timestamp"} 107→ optional_fields = {"plan_file"} 108→ 109→ # Check required fields exist 110→ missing = required_fields - set(data.keys()) 111→ if missing: 112→ raise ValueError(f"Missing required fields: {', '.join(sorted(missing))}") 113→ 114→ # Validate required fields 115→ if not isinstance(data["pr_number"], int): 116→ raise ValueError("pr_number must be an integer") 117→ if data["pr_number"] <= 0: 118→ raise ValueError("pr_number must be positive") 119→ 120→ if not isinstance(data["worktree_name"], str): 121→ raise ValueError("worktree_name must be a string") 122→ if len(data["worktree_name"]) == 0: 123→ raise ValueError("worktree_name must not be empty") 124→ 125→ if not isinstance(data["timestamp"], str): 126→ raise ValueError("timestamp must be a string") 127→ if len(data["timestamp"]) == 0: 128→ raise ValueError("timestamp must not be empty") 129→
... [170 lines omitted] ...
133→ raise ValueError("plan_file must be a string") 135→ raise ValueError("plan_file must not be empty") 141→ raise ValueError(f"Unknown fields: {', '.join(sorted(unknown_fields))}") 168→ raise ValueError(f"Missing required fields: {', '.join(sorted(missing))}") 172→ raise ValueError(f"Invalid status '{data['status']}'. Must be 'queued'") 176→ raise ValueError("queued_at must be a string") 178→ raise ValueError("queued_at must not be empty") 181→ raise ValueError("submitted_by must be a string") 183→ raise ValueError("submitted_by must not be empty") 186→ raise ValueError("expected_workflow must be a string") 188→ raise ValueError("expected_workflow must not be empty") 191→ raise ValueError("trigger_mechanism must be a string") 193→ raise ValueError("trigger_mechanism must not be empty") 197→ raise ValueError("pr_number must be an integer") 199→ raise ValueError("pr_number must be positive") 203→ raise ValueError("validation_results must be a dict") 229→ raise ValueError(f"Missing required fields: {', '.join(sorted(missing))}") 233→ raise ValueError(f"Invalid status '{data['status']}'.
